title: Quality gate: byte-identical rerun determinism test
description: tests/test_determinism.py: runs generate_test_suite.py twice to different output directories, asserts diff -r is empty. Must also assert PDF byte-identical (hits the reportlab invariant and fpdf2 creation_date gotcha from the stack decision). Runs in CI and in loop.sh --include-tests phase 2. Acceptance: test passes on an empty generator; will catch any nondeterminism as formatters are added.

title: Architecture: two-phase generator (canonical model + view formatters)
description:
Architectural decision that shapes how .1 / .2 / .3 / .4 and all 18 TCs are implemented.

## Problem
Cross-referential integrity across 18 test cases is fragile if each TC owns its own input generation. TC-01's trial balance must tie to TC-06's tax provision pre-tax book income must tie to TC-11's QofE EBITDA. Hand-maintaining these relationships across beads is where the ~5–10 'integration fix' iterations in the loop budget come from.

## Decision
Two-phase generator architecture.

### Phase 1: Canonical model (one-shot, deterministic)
Build the entire Cascade Industries universe as in-memory Python objects in a single seeded pass:

- Entities (parent + 3 subs)
- ChartOfAccounts (~120 accounts)
- Employees (850 rows)
- GeneralLedger (every JE for FY2023-FY2025, transaction-level)
- IntercompanyLedger (IC transactions, recorded on both sides → nets to zero on consolidation by construction)
- Leases (15 with all terms)
- Customers, Vendors, APTransactions (52K AP rows)
- K1Investments (8 K-1s)
- BankTransactions (340 bank rows + matching GL subset)
- RDProjects, RDTimeRecords (45 employees × 52 weeks)
- MonthlyRevenueByProduct

Derived artifacts (trial balance, financial statements, monthly P&L, AR aging, AP aging, DTA/DTL rollforward, tax provision, IC eliminations, intercompany margins) are computed as pure functions over the model. NEVER hand-entered.

### Phase 2: Test-case formatters (pure views)
Each TC has a tc_XX_formatter.py that consumes the canonical model and emits TC-specific files. Formatters may:
- Project/filter/aggregate model data
- Apply presentation chaos (merged cells, messy headers, abbreviations, inconsistent date formats, format changes across TCs)
- Apply planted errors from error_registry via a deterministic transformation layer

Formatters MAY NOT invent numbers. Any dollar amount in any TC file must trace to the canonical model.

### Phase 3: Gold standards (free)
gold_standards/TC-XX_gold.json is computed from the same canonical model by a third pure function. No hand-maintained expected values. Since the input file is 'model view + planted errors' and the gold standard is 'model view', the planted errors are the only difference — which is exactly what the agent under test is supposed to catch.

## Planted errors (new layering)
error_registry.json becomes a list of transformations: (file, location, transformation_fn, original_value, corrupted_value). Unit test: apply the error, un-apply, assert equals clean formatter output.

## Consequences
- Kills cross-referential drift by construction
- Eliminates ~15 iterations of gold standard hand-writing (bead .24 becomes trivial)
- Eliminates ~5-10 'integration fix' iterations
- Determinism quality gate becomes easier: one seeded model build, pure formatters
- Refactoring a TC's file format never changes the numbers, only the presentation

## Affects
- .1 Generator infrastructure — restructure around model/ + formatters/ + golds/ subpackages
- .2 Shared data files — shared_data/ files are just formatter outputs over the model
- .3 Master data model — the canonical model IS this bead
- .4 Canary + error registries — error registry format changes to the transformation layer above
- .24 Gold standards — becomes a ~1 iteration task (write the golds/ function that pickles model views)
- All 18 TC beads — acceptance now includes 'formatter must not invent numbers; must trace every value to model'

## Directory layout
synth-data/
  generator/
    model/           # Phase 1: canonical model + derived views
      entities.py
      gl.py
      intercompany.py
      tax.py
      ...
    formatters/      # Phase 2: per-TC file emission
      tc01_trial_balance.py
      tc06_tax_provision.py
      ...
    golds/           # Phase 3: gold standard emission
      tc01_gold.py
      ...
    errors.py        # Error transformation layer
    canaries.py
    manifest.py
  generate_test_suite.py  # orchestrator: build model → run formatters → run golds → emit registries
close_reason: Decision recorded. Two-phase generator architecture (canonical model + pure formatters + derived gold standards) is now the baseline. Description preserved for reference by all dependent work.

title: Model: GL posting engine (JE + balance checks)
description: generator/model/gl.py: JournalEntry dataclass (date, entity, description, lines=[(account, debit, credit, memo)]), Ledger class with post() that validates balanced entries. Query API: balance_by_account(entity, as_of_date), filter_by_date_range, filter_by_entity. No actual entries posted yet — this is the engine. Acceptance: unit test posts a balanced JE and retrieves the balance; posting an unbalanced JE raises.

title: Model: revenue + COGS generators (seasonal, per-entity margins)
description: generator/model/revenue.py: monthly revenue by product × entity × month for FY2023-FY2025 with seasonal patterns (Q4 heavy, Q1 dip — not uniform). Apply growth rates from prompt.md §2.4 (FY23→24 +6%, FY24→25 +9% with Advanced Materials driving growth). Cascade Advanced Materials has one product line that declines 4% (for TC-03 detection). Consolidated FY25 growth must equal 9.2% (TC-03 gold value). COGS correlated at 35%/52%/18% gross margins per entity. Posts to GL via gl.post(). Acceptance: monthly totals sum to entity-level revenue targets from §1.2; consolidated YoY growth = 9.2%.

title: Model: 850-employee roster
description: generator/model/employees.py: deterministic generation of 850 employees per prompt.md §1.4 (employee_id, name via seeded Faker, entity, department, title, hire_date with 3-year distribution, annual_salary by title×location, state, cost_center, is_r&d_eligible, termination_date with ~8% annual churn). R&D eligibility only on Advanced Materials R&D and Engineering staff. Acceptance: unit test asserts 850 rows, 45 R&D-eligible at Advanced Materials (needed by TC-08), ~8% termination rate, byte-identical across reruns.

title: Quality gate: every canary findable in its file
description: tests/test_canaries.py: after running the generator, load canary_registry.json, and for each entry open the referenced file and verify the canary is present at the expected location (xlsx custom property / docx custom property / pdf metadata / csv comment). Acceptance: test green after foundation; test red if any formatter forgets to embed its canary.

title: Model: opex, fixed assets, depreciation (MACRS + straight-line)
description: generator/model/opex.py + generator/model/ppe.py: SG&A by department, R&D spend ~12% of Advanced Materials revenue, payroll derived from employee roster. Fixed asset register with acquisition dates and book vs tax depreciation (MACRS vs straight-line — creates the depreciation temp difference used in TC-06 tax provision). Posts to GL. Acceptance: Advanced Materials R&D expense ≈ 12% of its revenue; book-tax depreciation difference exposed as a queryable view for TC-06.

title: Model: bank transactions (340 rows + GL cash subset for TC-02)
description: generator/model/bank.py: 340 bank transactions for December 2025 + matching GL cash detail. Planted: 4 outstanding checks clearing in January, 2 deposits in transit. Bank confirmation balance derivable. Per TC-02 gold: adjusted bank balance and adjusted book balance both agree at $4,287,331. Confirmation letter balance $4,312,117. Fuzzy-match attributes (dates ±2bd, exact amount). Acceptance: reconciliation computed by the model matches TC-02 gold exactly.

title: Model: intercompany ledger (cost-plus-8%, fees, IC loan)
description: generator/model/intercompany.py per prompt.md §1.3: Precision → Advanced at cost-plus-8%, Distribution warehouse fees at market rate, management fees at 1.5% of sub revenue from parent, $5M IC loan parent → Advanced at 5%. Every IC transaction recorded on both sides so consolidation eliminates to zero by construction. Services transaction at 11.2% margin (outside IQR — TC-09 flag). Acceptance: on consolidation, all 9xxx IC accounts sum to zero; services margin = 11.2%.

title: Model: AR and AP subledgers + aging
description: generator/model/ar.py + generator/model/ap.py: AR subledger derived from revenue (customer-level, with DSO distribution producing realistic aging buckets current/30/60/90/120+), bad debt allowance and write-off history. AP subledger with vendor-level balances by aging bucket. These feed TC-05 AR memo, TC-14 cash flow, TC-13 forensic AP. Acceptance: AR aging totals sum to AR balance on trial balance; AP aging totals sum to AP balance.

title: Canary generator and registry emitter
description: generator/canaries.py: deterministic 8-char alphanumeric canary generator (seeded from config), registry dataclass mapping file→canary→location, JSON emitter. Helper to embed a canary into xlsx metadata, docx custom property, PDF metadata, CSV comment line. Acceptance: unit test asserts canaries are unique, deterministic across runs, and findable in each file type.

title: Planted error transformation framework
description: generator/errors.py: PlantedError dataclass (error_id, file, location, type, description, severity, test_cases) and transformation functions for each error type in prompt.md §1.7 (transpose_digit, mismatch_total, stale_data, formula_error, wrong_entity, date_inconsistency, classification_error, missing_data, rounding). Each transformation is (clean_value → corrupted_value). Registry emitter to error_registry.json. Acceptance: unit test applies each error type and verifies corrupted value matches expected.

title: Quality gate: all generated files open cleanly
description: tests/test_file_integrity.py: walks manifest.json, opens every xlsx with openpyxl, every docx with python-docx, every pdf with pypdf, every csv with stdlib csv, every sqlite with sqlite3 — no exceptions. Catches corrupt files and format violations before the auto_grader sees them.

title: Model: chart of accounts (~120 accounts)
description: generator/model/coa.py: Account dataclass (number, name, type, normal_balance, description), ~120 accounts covering 1xxx Assets / 2xxx Liabilities / 3xxx Equity / 4xxx Revenue / 5xxx COGS / 6xxx Opex / 7xxx Other / 8xxx Tax / 9xxx IC per prompt.md §1.5. Granular R&D expense accounts (required by TC-08). Separate lease liability accounts (required by TC-04 ASC 842). Acceptance: account list validates (unique numbers, correct type prefixes, both sides of every IC pair present).

title: Model: 15 leases with terms (for TC-04 and ASC 842 adjustments)
description: generator/model/leases.py: 15 leases with lessee/lessor, commencement date, term, base rent, escalation (fixed % / CPI / stepped), renewal options, purchase options. 2 qualify for short-term exemption (TC-04 gold). 3 have amendments that materially change terms. Compute ROU asset and lease liability per ASC 842 — these feed the temp difference in TC-06. Post lease expense and ASC 842 entries to GL. Acceptance: lease schedule export matches TC-04 gold; ASC 842 temp difference exposed for TC-06.

title: Orchestrator skeleton: generate_test_suite.py CLI entry point
description: Top-level script with argparse (--config, --output), calls config loader, seeds random/numpy/faker from config, creates empty output directory tree matching prompt.md §2.3. No model or formatters wired yet — just the pipeline shape. Acceptance: python generate_test_suite.py --output /tmp/x creates the expected directory tree and exits 0.

title: Model: consolidation engine + IC eliminations
description: generator/model/consolidation.py: consolidate the 4 entities into 'consolidated' view, applying IC eliminations. Consolidated revenue ≈ $200M per prompt.md §1.1. Balance sheet balances (assets = liabilities + equity). Cash flow derived from BS changes (indirect method). Acceptance: consolidated revenue in $198-202M range; BS balances for all 3 years; all IC accounts zero out.

title: Model: entities constants (parent + 3 subsidiaries)
description: generator/model/entities.py: dataclass Entity (code, name, location, state, revenue_target, gross_margin, revenue_mix). Constants for parent Cascade Industries Inc. + Cascade Precision Components LLC + Cascade Advanced Materials Inc. + Cascade Distribution Services LLC. Values come directly from prompt.md §1.1-1.2. Acceptance: entities module exports a dict keyed by entity code with all four entities populated per spec.

title: Config schema: config.yaml + loader with validation
description: Define config.yaml schema (seed, output_dir, company_params with growth rates and margins, canary assignments, error injection points). Write generator/config.py that loads and validates via pyyaml. Acceptance: loading a malformed config raises a clear error; default config round-trips cleanly.

title: Gold standard emission framework
description: generator/golds/framework.py: generic gold standard emitter. Each per-TC gold is a thin function that consumes the model and emits TC-XX_gold.json (expected numerical values, structural requirements, canary verifications, error detection expectations per prompt.md gold standard schemas). Includes self-test hook: an auto_grader run against gold-produced reference outputs must score 3/3/3/3/3. Acceptance: framework can round-trip a sample gold (emit → re-read → equal).

title: Quality gate: every planted error verifiable in its file
description: tests/test_errors.py: loads error_registry.json, verifies each planted error is present in its referenced file with the corrupted value, and that applying the inverse transformation yields the clean value from the model. Acceptance: all 25 errors round-trip.

title: Model: tax provision (perm/temp differences, DTAs/DTLs, ETR)
description: generator/model/tax.py per prompt.md TC-06: pre-tax book income from GL, permanent differences (M&E 50%, tax-exempt interest, stock comp excess, fines), temporary differences (depreciation book vs tax, ASC 842 lease adjustments, warranty reserve, inventory reserve, accrued bonuses, bad debt reserve). DTA/DTL rollforward FY2024→FY2025. Federal 21% + blended state 6.2%. Target effective tax rate ≈ 24.8%. Acceptance: computed ETR within 0.1% of 24.8%, current+deferred ties to total provision.

title: Model: derived views (trial balance, financial statements, monthly P&L)
description: generator/model/views.py: pure functions producing trial balance (entity×account×period), balance sheet, income statement, cash flow (indirect), monthly P&L by account. All derived from GL — never hand-entered. These are the inputs that Phase 2 formatters consume. Acceptance: unit tests for every view; trial balance for FY25 consolidated sums to $0 (debits=credits); monthly P&L aggregates match annual IS.

title: Project scaffolding: pyproject.toml, uv lockfile, directory layout, ruff+pytest config
description: Create pyproject.toml with Python 3.11+ constraint and the locked stack (openpyxl, python-docx, reportlab, fpdf2, pypdf, pandas, numpy, faker, pyyaml, pytest, ruff). Run uv lock to pin versions. Create directory layout: generator/{model,formatters,golds}/, tests/, scoring/, templates/. Configure ruff and pytest in pyproject.toml. Acceptance: uv sync succeeds; uv run ruff check . and uv run pytest both exit 0 on empty project.

title: Templates directory: 4 template files for TC-05, TC-16, TC-17
description: Generate templates/: workpaper_memo_template.docx (TC-05), engagement_letter_template.docx (TC-16 with <<field_name>> merge fields), deliverable_cover_page.docx (TC-17), formatting_guide.pdf (TC-17 — section order, page numbering rules Roman/Arabic, header/footer, fonts). These are static templates consumed by TCs, not generated data. See prompt.md §2.3 output directory structure.
close_reason: Superseded by finer-grained breakdown under the two-phase architecture. Work is now tracked in leaf beads under the epic.

title: Tech stack decision: Python 3.11+ with pinned office-file libraries
description:
Language: Python 3.11+ (spec mandates it in §2.1/§2.2).

Pinned stack:
- openpyxl — xlsx read/write (formulas, merged cells, formatting)
- python-docx — docx (styles, headers, tables)
- reportlab — text-native PDFs
- fpdf2 — scanned-style PDFs (low-res image output)
- pypdf — PDF read-back for auto-grader
- pandas + numpy — financial data generation, 52K-row AP ledger, Benford analysis
- faker — synthetic names/addresses (with fixed seed)
- pyyaml — config.yaml
- pytest — generator + grader tests
- uv (or poetry) — lockfile for reproducibility

Determinism rules (required by §9 quality gate: byte-identical reruns):
- random.seed(42) + numpy.random.default_rng(42)
- Avoid unordered set iteration; sort dict keys before writing
- Faker: Faker.seed(42) and per-instance seeding
- Lock all dependency versions via lockfile

PDF DETERMINISM GOTCHA: reportlab embeds timestamps and producer strings by default, which breaks byte-identical reruns. Must set invariant=True on Canvas and pin a fixed creation date in document metadata. fpdf2 has a similar issue — set creation_date and modification_date explicitly. Verify this early by running the generator twice and diffing the PDF outputs; do not wait until the §9 quality gate to discover it.
close_reason: Decision recorded. Stack is locked: Python 3.11+, openpyxl/python-docx/reportlab/fpdf2/pypdf/pandas/numpy/faker/pyyaml/pytest/uv. Determinism rules and PDF timestamp gotcha documented in description for reference by dependent beads.

title: Gold standards for all 18 test cases
description: For each TC: JSON with expected numerical values and structural requirements, plus reference output files (xlsx/docx) where applicable. Gold standards must themselves score 3/3/3/3/3 when run through auto_grader. See prompt.md §9.
close_reason: Superseded by finer-grained breakdown under the two-phase architecture.

title: README + quality gates (byte-identical reruns, canary/error verification)
description: Setup and execution instructions. Quality gates: generate_test_suite.py emits byte-identical outputs on rerun; every canary findable in its file; every error verifiable; all xlsx/docx/pdf open cleanly; manual walkthrough of 3 test cases. See prompt.md §9 quality gates.
close_reason: Superseded by finer-grained breakdown under the two-phase architecture.

title: Scoring system: rubrics.yaml, auto_grader.py, scoring_template.xlsx
description:
Per-test-case 5-dimension rubric (Correctness/Completeness/Format/Robustness/Communication, 1-3 scale). Pass ≥2.4 avg, Conditional ≥2.0 + no 1s, Fail <2.0 or any 1.

Deliverables:
- rubrics.yaml — scoring criteria per TC
- auto_grader.py — validates numerical outputs vs gold, checks canaries, detects planted errors, outputs JSON per TC + aggregate
- scoring_template.xlsx — 4 sheets:
  1. Scorecard (1 row per TC, 5 dimension columns + notes)
  2. Grader Instructions (per-dimension, per-TC guidance)
  3. Inter-Rater Agreement (2 raters' scores + Cohen's kappa computation)
  4. Aggregate Dashboard — capability matrix pivots on 3 axes:
     Axis 1: Service Line (Audit, Tax, Advisory, Cross-Service)
     Axis 2: Capability (File Reading, File Writing, Data Analysis/SQL/DataFrame, RAG, Multi-Step Workflow)
     Axis 3: Difficulty (Routine, Complex, Adversarial)
     Each TC maps to ≥1 cell. Dashboard shows pass rates by each combination.

See prompt.md §7.1-§7.5.
close_reason: Superseded by finer-grained breakdown under the two-phase architecture.

title: Canary registry and 25-error registry
description: Unique 8-char canary in every file (some cross-referential across test cases). Plant exactly 25 deliberate errors across specified types (transposed digits, mismatched totals, stale data, formula errors, wrong entity names, inconsistent dates, classification errors, missing data, rounding). Both registries emitted as JSON. See prompt.md §1.6, §1.7.
close_reason: Superseded by finer-grained breakdown under the two-phase architecture.

title: Generator infrastructure: config.yaml, generate_test_suite.py, manifest
description: Deterministic (SEED=42) Python generator that reads config.yaml and emits the full corpus. Produces manifest.json listing every file with path/type/size/canary/test cases. Must be byte-identical across runs. See prompt.md §2.1, §2.2.
close_reason: Superseded by finer-grained breakdown under the two-phase architecture. Work is now tracked in leaf beads under the epic.

title: Shared data files (COA, employee roster, org chart, financials, intercompany)
description: Generate shared_data/: master_coa.xlsx, employee_roster.csv, entity_org_chart.pdf, cascade_consolidated_financials_fy2023/24/25.xlsx, intercompany_transactions.xlsx. Financial realism rules: seasonal revenue, correlated COGS, balancing BS, indirect-method CF, netting intercompany, growth FY23→24 +6% / FY24→25 +9%. See prompt.md §1.4, §1.5, §2.3, §2.4.
close_reason: Superseded by finer-grained breakdown under the two-phase architecture.

title: Master data model: Cascade Industries entities, COA, employees
description: Define the single source of truth: parent + 3 subsidiaries, intercompany relationships, 850-employee roster, ~120-account unified chart of accounts, 3 years of financial history (FY2023-FY2025). All downstream test cases reference this data. See prompt.md §1.
close_reason: Superseded by finer-grained breakdown under the two-phase architecture. Work is now tracked in leaf beads under the epic.

title: Build Big 4 agent test suite (Cascade Industries)
description: Complete qualitative test suite for AI agent operating on Big 4 professional services workloads (Audit, Tax, Advisory). Generates 18 test cases grounded in a single fictional company (Cascade Industries) with cross-referential integrity. See prompt.md for the full specification.

title: Scoring: auto_grader.py (mechanical dimensions)
description: scoring/auto_grader.py with TestCaseGrader class per prompt.md §7.2: grade_correctness (numerical compare with ±0.5% tolerance), grade_completeness (required files/sheets/sections), grade_format (file validity), verify_canaries (reading correct files), check_error_detection (flagged errors). JSON report per TC + aggregate summary. --self-test runs grader against gold-produced outputs and asserts 3/3/3/3/3. Acceptance: --self-test green.

title: Model: R&D projects (12) + time records (45 emp × 52 weeks) for TC-08
description: generator/model/rd.py per TC-08: 12 R&D projects (8 qualify under 4-part test, 2 borderline, 2 don't — e.g. market research labeled R&D). ~2,340 time record rows (45 R&D-eligible employees × 52 weeks with multiple project allocations per week). Supply expenses coded to R&D cost centers. Compute QREs. ASC credit ≈ $185K. Prior year QREs: FY2023 $3.1M, FY2024 $3.4M. Acceptance: credit within $500 of $185K gold.

title: Model: 52K-row AP transaction ledger with 7 planted anomaly categories
description: generator/model/ap_ledger.py per TC-13: 52,000 AP transactions with all 7 anomaly categories embedded at known transaction IDs: 4 exact dup + 4 near-dup (total exposure $127,340), 35 txn cluster $9,900-$9,999, 12 round-number payments to Pacific Consulting Group, 15 weekend/holiday approvals, 8 invoice-after-payment, 2 similar-name vendors (JKL Services LLC/Inc), 1 vendor at employee home address (PC-0342), 3 split transactions, 1 self-approved, 1 single-approver cost center. Each anomaly indexed by transaction_id for gold standard. Acceptance: TC-13 gold findable by filtering the ledger on anomaly flags.

title: Model: 8 K-1 investments (for TC-07)
description: generator/model/k1.py: 8 partnership investments with K-1 box data (1-13 plus Box 20 codes). Amounts $5K to $2.3M. One marked amended: ordinary income 340K → 285K, adds $55K guaranteed payment. Section 199A amounts present but flagged N/A to C-corp. Acceptance: consolidated totals match TC-07 gold exactly.

title: Template: cover page docx + formatting_guide.pdf (for TC-17)
description: templates/deliverable_cover_page.docx with branded cover + title/date placeholders. templates/formatting_guide.pdf specifying section order, page numbering rules (Roman for front matter, Arabic for body), header/footer requirements, font standards. Acceptance: both files open; formatting guide readable as text-native PDF.

title: Template: workpaper_memo_template.docx (for TC-05)
description: Create templates/workpaper_memo_template.docx with firm-standard sections: Objective, Scope, Procedures, Findings, Conclusion. Headers/footers with merge fields, firm branding styles. Hand-crafted static file committed to repo (not regenerated). Acceptance: opens in Word/LibreOffice; python-docx can read sections.

title: Manifest.json emitter
description: generator/manifest.py: tracks every file emitted by the generator (path, type, size, canary, which test cases use it) and writes manifest.json at the end of the run. Integrated into orchestrator as a context manager that formatters register files against. Acceptance: running the empty orchestrator with a dummy formatter produces a valid manifest.json.

title: Model: customers, vendors, contracts (for TC-11 QofE, TC-12 data room)
description: generator/model/customers.py: top customers (top customer = 18% of revenue per TC-11), 8 contracts with terms/volumes/renewal dates (including one change-of-control clause for TC-12 Acme). Vendors from AP ledger. Key people for TC-12 data room (CEO golden parachute 3× salary, CFO, CTO employment agreements). Pending litigation $2.5M exposure. Acceptance: top 10 customer concentration, change-of-control flags, litigation data all queryable from model.

title: Scoring: rubrics.yaml (5 dimensions × 18 test cases)
description: scoring/rubrics.yaml: per-TC rubric on the 5 dimensions (Correctness, Completeness, Format Compliance, Robustness, Communication) from prompt.md §7.1. Score 3/2/1 anchors per dimension per test case with concrete value references to the gold standards. Acceptance: yaml loads, every TC has all 5 dimensions, every dimension has 3/2/1 anchors.

title: Template: engagement_letter_template.docx with merge fields (for TC-16)
description: Create templates/engagement_letter_template.docx with <<client_name>>, <<engagement_scope>>, <<fee_amount>>, <<payment_terms>>, <<start_date>>, <<partner_name>> placeholders. Standard engagement letter language. Acceptance: python-docx can enumerate the merge fields.

title: TC-18: Prior year workpaper rollforward (Cross-service, Adversarial)
description: 10 prior year workpapers (6 xlsx, 4 docx) + current year data with deliberate format changes (TB is now CSV, projections now docx) + new goodwill impairment xlsx. Update 8/10 files cleanly, flag 2 requiring judgment (format-changed projections, planning memo rewrite), flag goodwill as significant new audit area. See prompt.md TC-18.

title: TC-17: Multi-file deliverable assembly (Cross-service, Complex)
description: 6 workpaper sections (mix xlsx/docx) + cover page template + formatting guide PDF. Assemble with TOC, Roman numeral front matter + Arabic body, consistent formatting, embedded tables from Excel sections. Output: single PDF deliverable to C-suite standard. See prompt.md TC-17.

title: TC-15: DCF valuation (Advisory, Complex)
description: 3yr historicals + 5yr mgmt projections + 10 trading comps + 15-page industry PDF. Build UFCF, derive WACC ≈10.8%, compute terminal value via Gordon Growth + exit multiple, sensitivity tables. Target EV $245-305M (GG) / $260-320M (EM), equity ≈$255M. Flag 200bps margin expansion as aggressive. Output: DCF xlsx + summary docx. See prompt.md TC-15.

title: TC-11: Quality of earnings (Advisory, Complex)
description: 36 months P&L + mgmt adjustments (with 2 recurring ones to challenge + aggressive run-rate) + 8 customer contracts + mgmt interview notes. Compute EBITDA, challenge aggressive adjustments, target adjusted EBITDA ≈$29.3M, flag 18% top-customer concentration. Output: analysis workbook xlsx + Key Findings docx. See prompt.md TC-11.

title: TC-12: Data room triage & document index (Advisory, Adversarial)
description: 32-file data room across 7 categories + 65-item DD checklist. Produce document index, gap analysis, red-flag memo. Plant: product liability lawsuit ($2.5M exposure), CEO golden parachute (3× salary), Acme change-of-control, 2 expiring patents, incomplete IP assignments, missing environmental/permits/claims/surveys. See prompt.md TC-12.

title: TC-13: Forensic AP transaction analysis (Advisory, Complex)
description: 52,000-row AP CSV with 7 planted anomaly categories (duplicates, Benford cluster near $10K threshold, round-number shell vendor, weekend approvals, invoice-after-payment, similar vendors, employee-address vendor PC-0342, split transactions, self-approval, single-approver concentration). Duplicate exposure $127,340. Output: analysis xlsx + executive memo docx. See prompt.md TC-13.

title: TC-09: Transfer pricing documentation (Tax, Complex)
description: Intercompany transactions + 12 comparable companies (2 should be rejected) + prior year TP report PDF. Compute benchmarked IQR (4.2%-8.7%), check Cascade's margins, flag services transaction at 11.2% outside range. Output: benchmarking xlsx + updated results section docx. See prompt.md TC-09.

title: TC-06: Tax provision under ASC 740 (Tax, Complex)
description: Consolidated TB + prior-year provision workpaper + perm/temp differences docx + statutory rates. Compute current + deferred provision, roll forward DTAs/DTLs, ETR reconciliation. Expected ETR ~24.8%. Output: xlsx with Current/Deferred Rollforward/Rate Rec/Summary sheets. See prompt.md TC-06.

title: TC-07: K-1 extraction & consolidation (Tax, Adversarial)
description: 8 K-1 PDFs (3 clean system-generated, 5 with varying layouts/fonts/column widths from different partnership preparers, 1 amended). Extract all box items, map to 1120 lines, catch amended K-1 (ordinary income 340→285K + $55K guaranteed payment), flag Section 199A N/A to C-corp. See prompt.md TC-07.

title: TC-08: R&D tax credit study - Section 41 (Tax, Complex)
description: 2,340-row weekly time records + 12 project description docx + payroll register + supply expenses. Apply 4-part test (8 qualify, 2 borderline, 2 don't), compute QREs, ASC method credit ≈$185K. Output: qualification analysis, QRE calc, credit calc (xlsx) + contemporaneous memo (docx). See prompt.md TC-08.

title: TC-01: Trial balance reconciliation (Audit, Complex)
description: Messy FY2025 TB vs prior year workpaper + signed FY2024 PDF. Agent must map accounts, compute variances, tie-out to signed financials, catch transposed-digit error ERR-001. Deliverable: xlsx with Mapping/Variance Analysis/Exceptions/Tie-Out sheets. See prompt.md TC-01.

title: TC-02: Bank reconciliation & confirmation matching (Audit, Complex)
description: 340-row bank CSV + GL cash detail + bank confirmation PDF. Fuzzy-match transactions (±2 business days, exact amount), identify outstanding checks/deposits in transit, tie to confirmation. Adjusted balances must agree at $4,287,331. See prompt.md TC-02.

title: TC-04: Lease extraction & ASC 842 schedule (Audit, Adversarial)
description: 15 lease PDFs (10 text-native, 3 scanned-style, 2 with amendments) + partially populated lease schedule. Extract terms, use amended terms where present, flag short-term exemptions (2 qualify), flag OCR uncertainty on scanned docs. See prompt.md TC-04.

title: TC-03: Substantive analytical procedures on revenue (Audit, Complex)
description: 24 months revenue-by-product + 12-page industry benchmark PDF (benchmarks on pp. 4/7/11) + mgmt rep letter asserting ~8% growth. Agent must detect actual 9.2% and challenge the representation. Output: memo (docx) + analysis workbook (xlsx). See prompt.md TC-03.

title: Scoring: scoring_template.xlsx (human grading + kappa + dashboard)
description: scoring/scoring_template.xlsx per §7.3: Sheet 1 Scorecard (row per TC, columns for each dimension + notes), Sheet 2 Grader Instructions (per-dimension per-TC guidance), Sheet 3 Inter-Rater Agreement (two raters, Cohen's kappa formula), Sheet 4 Aggregate Dashboard (pivot tables by service line × capability × difficulty per §7.5 capability matrix). Built programmatically with openpyxl so it's regenerable.

title: README with setup and execution instructions
description: README.md covering: what the suite is, prerequisites (Python 3.11+, uv), uv sync, running the generator (python generate_test_suite.py), running the auto-grader, test execution protocol per prompt.md §8, regression workflow per §8.4. Acceptance: a new contributor can clone and run the full suite using only README instructions.

title: TC-16: Engagement letter generation (Cross-service, Routine)
description: Client profile + fee schedule xlsx + engagement letter docx template with merge fields. Look up fees by tier/entity count, apply 1.15x IPO complexity multiplier (audit $327,750), add $12K per-entity tax adder × 3 ($131,000), total $458,750. Populate template with Sarah Chen, March 15 2026 start, Net 30. Output: docx. See prompt.md TC-16.

title: TC-14: 13-week cash flow forecast (Advisory, Routine)
description: Current BS + AP/AR aging + committed/discretionary expenses. Project weekly cash, identify Week 8 trough at $1.34M (breaches $2M covenant), show discretionary deferrals (marketing + training from Week 5) bringing trough to $2.155M. Include 1-week collection slowdown sensitivity. Output: xlsx. See prompt.md TC-14.

title: TC-10: Multi-state apportionment (Tax, Routine)
description: Consolidated P&L + state factors (OR/TX/IL complete, CA/WA partial, NY blank) + apportionment rules doc. Distinguish zero vs missing, flag TX margin tax / WA B&O tax as different base, flag WA nexus question. Output: xlsx. See prompt.md TC-10.

title: TC-05: AR workpaper memo (Audit, Routine)
description: AR aging + confirmation summary + allowance analysis + firm memo template. Draft workpaper memo following Big 4 standards: objective, procedures, findings, conclusion. Output: docx. See prompt.md TC-05.
